EX 95 – Active Palletiz ation oLPNS Base WMOS does not inform the user that the pallet is ready to close and needing to end palletization. RF Palletize oLPN is enhanced to allow a configurable value to allow McLane to configure/customize the Maximum # of oLPNs on a pallet based on division requests. WM will display a custom message “ M aximum oLPNs /Totes allowed on Pallet ” which will show to the floor user that no more oLPNs /totes can fit onto the pallet – signaling to the user to end the current pallet and start a new pallet with the remaining oLPNs needing to be palletized. Additionally, McLane would like visibility on the number of oLPNs /Totes remaining on a shipment to be packed . WMOS is enhanced to allow user to invoke custom CTRL-KEY: CTRL- T to display a custom Pallet Tracking screen As part of the enhancement, WMOS displays the pallet tracking screen grouped by container type within a shipment using CTRL-KEY: CTRL-T . WMOS displays totals oLPNs needing to be pa lletized , already pa lletized oLPNs , the total number of pallets and the percentage of oLPNs palletized completed as part of additional screen changes. WMOS is customized to display custom Pallet tracking screens by container type within a shipment when user invokes custom control key from pallet prompt in Palletize oLPN transaction. The tracking screen should display: • Total number of totes to be palletized on the pallet. • Already palletized totes . • The total number of pallets needed for the shipment . • The percentage of oLPNs palletized . Additionally, WMOS is enhanced to allow users to invoke custom CTRL-KEY: CTRL-C to display custom Tracking screens based on the status of the oLPN /Tote and the oLPN (s)/Tote(s) that are in that status remaining in the container type of the shipment. WMOS also introduces a custom error message CUST9502: “Container Type does not match for Designated Pallet” to allow for only oLPNs with the same container type to be palletized onto the same pallet. Summary Information Customer Name: McLane Customer Code: MCLN Subproject Codes: TBD Change Request Number: Overview Functional Area Enhanced Additional Information User Interface (UI) N MHE Y RF Y RF Palletize oLPN – Custom validation for PTL Pallet Reporting N Other N Assumptions There will be only 1 shipment per pallet; however, the shipment can be spread out across multiple pallets. Totes are uniform in size when getting palletized onto a pallet. All oLPNs are in packed status before they are palletized . This is controlled in WMOS by configuring the Sort Divert Action for the oLPN status “Packed”. Sort divert action record is configured in WMOS for the combination of oLPN status “packed” and logical divert. De-palletization must be done manually through WMOS RF and UI options . Both oLPN barcode type and pallet barcode type are already configured in WMOS. For EX95 RF Palletize oLPNs Transaction Parameters assume the following configuration for 4-4 Auto Generate Pallet = Do not auto generate pallet . EX95 may only be invoked after one oLPN has been assigned to pallet. oLPNs will be numeric until assigned an XREF/GLP. All oLPNs have a valid container type. All totes on a pallet are the same container type. MCLN will handle container type configuration for display of EX95 tracking screens purposes . When transaction parameter 502-502 is disabled, EX95 will still display the container type of the first scanned oLPN on the custom tracking screens. A maximum threshold of 10 container type s can be set up within the system code 95A-EQV to allow same container type palletization. Dependencies Code Dependencies N/A Interface Dependencies Interface Field Requirement P latform Information Solution: Solution Platform: Solution Version: Warehouse Management Open Systems Oracle/ Tomcat /RHEL 201 9 Proces s Flow Diagram Process Steps : User scans a blind pallet ID in pallet prompt. User scans the oLPN ID/GLP on oLPN prompt wanting to put oLPNs (s)/GLP on pallet. WMOS checks if EX95 is enabled. If enabled, WMOS performs custom pallet validations on pallet. Otherwise, WMOS proceeds to next step per base. IF pallet has reached its max tote value C-E95-MAX. WMOS displays custom warning message CUST9501: “Maximum oLPNs /Totes allowed on Pallet”. WM will then automatically end the current pallet and prompt the user to scan the next blind pallet ID per base palletization. Otherwise, WMOS proceeds with next step. IF the scanned oLPNs container type is NOT the same as the first scanned oLPNs container type on the pallet. THEN WM throws custom error message CUST9502: “Container Type does not match for Designated Pallet ” and takes user back to the pallet prompt screen. Otherwise, WMOS proceeds with next step. WMOS determines destination locations for oLPNs in pallet per base. UI No UI changes in scope for this extension. RF RF Palletize oLPN Program Platform Available Text Based (RF Gun) Y UI N Mobile (DM) N Base RF Palletize oLPN transaction is enhanced to perform custom active pallet validations on PTL pallets . WMOS is enhanced to allow user to invoke custom CTRL-KEY: CTRL-T to display a custom Pallet Tracking screen. WMOS displays totals oLPNs needing to be palletized, already palletized oLPNs , the total number of pallets and the percentage of oLPNs palletized completed as part of additional screen changes under CTRL-T functionality. Additionally, WMOS is enhanced to allow users to invoke custom CTRL-KEY: CTRL-C to display custom Tracking screens based on the status of the oLPN /Tote and the oLPN (s)/Tote(s) that are in that status remaining in the container type of the shipment. An additional e nhancement allows a configurable value through system parameter C-E95-MAX to let McLane configure the Maximum # of oLPNs allowed on a pallet based on division requests. C- E 95-MAX will be leveraged in order to populate specific values on the custom Pallet Track Screen to better aide the floor users in determining the number of pallets needing to grab per each shipment. Enablement The following section describes the custom configuration required to enable this portion of the extension. First, the global modifications flag must be enabled. This is controlled through the custom system code C000 code id 000 parameter byte 1-1. This parameter is configured as a Y/N flag. If the flag is set to ‘Y’, then WMOS continues to the next enablement validation. Otherwise, if this flag is set to ‘N’, the global extension control is turned off, and only base processing will occur. Second, the extension specific global flag must be enabled. This is controlled through the custom system code C000 code id EX 95 byte 1-1. This parameter is configured as a Y/N flag. If the flag is set to ‘Y’, then WMOS continues to the custom processing below. Otherwise, if the flag is set to ‘N’, the extension specific control for EX 95 is turned off, and no custom EX 95 processing will occur. Third, the extension specific MAX value must be configured . This is controlled through the custom system code C -E95 code id MAX byte 1- 2 . This parameter is configured as a n edit box . If the edit box is configured , then WMOS continues to the custom processing below. Otherwise, if the edit box is ‘NULL’ or ’0' , and the extension specific MAX value for EX95 is turned off, and no custom EX95 processing will occur. Lastly, RF Palletize oLPNs Transaction Parameter 501 - 501 must be enabled. This parameter is configured as a Y/N flag. If the flag is set to ‘Y’, then WMOS continues with custom processing below. Otherwise, if the flag is set to ‘N’, the extension specific control for EX95 is turned off, and no custom EX95 processing will occur. The custom warning message is CUST9501: "Maximum oLPNs /Totes allowed on Pallet" . This message will be displayed when the pallet has exceeded the max tote value C-E95-MAX . This message indicate s to the floor user that no more oLPNs /totes can fit onto the current pallet . Once the custom message is accepted, then WMOS automatically end s the current pallet and then prompt s the user for the base pallet prompt screen . This will allow the user to begin a new pallet with the remaining oLPNs needing to be palletized. During the RF Palletize oLPN process, there is second custom message which will give information to the user. The custom message is CUST9502: “Container Type does not match for Designated Pallet.” This message will be displayed when the scanned oLPN does not match the first oLPN scanned onto the pallet. This message indicates to the floor user that the GLP/Tote does not belong onto the pallet as there are differing container types. Once the custom message is accepted, WMOS then prompts the user for the base pallet prompt screen. This will allow the user to begin a new pallet or scan an already existing pallet with the oLPNs needing to be palletized correctly. Enablement The following section describes the custom configuration required to enable this portion of the extension. First, the global modifications flag must be enabled. This is controlled through the custom system code C000 code id 000 parameter byte 1-1. This parameter is configured as a Y/N flag. If the flag is set to ‘Y’, then WMOS continues to the next enablement validation. Otherwise, if this flag is set to ‘N’, the global extension control is turned off, and only base processing will occur. Second, the extension specific global flag must be enabled. This is controlled through the custom system code C000 code id EX95 byte 1-1. This parameter is configured as a Y/N flag. If the flag is set to ‘Y’, then WMOS continues to the custom processing below. Otherwise, if the flag is set to ‘N’, the extension specific control for EX95 is turned off, and no custom EX95 processing will occur. Third, the extension specific MAX value must be configured. This is controlled through the custom system code C-E95 code id MAX byte 1-2. This parameter is configured as an edit box. If the edit box is configured, then WMOS continues to the custom processing below. Otherwise, if the edit box is ‘NULL’ or ’0', and the extension specific MAX value for EX95 is turned off, and no custom EX95 processing will occur. Next, RF Palletize oLPNs Transaction Parameter 500-500 must be enabled. This parameter is configured as a Y/N flag. If the flag is set to ‘Y’, then WMOS continues with custom processing below and display CUST9501: "Maximum oLPNs /Totes allowed on Pallet" when applicable. Otherwise, if the flag is set to ‘N’, the extension specific control for EX95 is turned off, and no custom EX95 processing will occur. Next, RF Palletize oLPNs Transaction Parameter 50 2 -50 2 must be enabled. This parameter is configured as a Y/N flag. If the flag is set to ‘Y’, then WMOS continues with custom processing below and display CUST9502: “Container Type does not match for Designated Pallet.” when applicable . Otherwise, if the flag is set to ‘N’, the extension specific control for EX95 is turned off, and no custom EX95 processing will occur. Validations The following section describes the validations performed by WMOS when the custom palletize oLPN message is received.    WMOS leverages base RF Palletize oLPN program to validate the oLPN and pallet. WMOS performs base validations against received pallet number. WMOS also performs base validations for oLPN . ` IF the total count of oLPNs on the pallet is greater than the configured Maximum number of oLPNs allowed on the pallet. THEN WM throws custom warning message CUST9501: “Maximum oLPNs /Totes allowed on Pallet” and takes user back to the pallet prompt screen. ELSE User has not reached the maximum number of oLPNs and user continues to Palletize oLPNs . IF the scanned oLPNs container type is NOT the same as the first scanned oLPNs container type on the pallet THEN WM throws custom error message CUST9502: “Container Type does not match for Designated Pallet ” and takes user back to the pallet prompt screen. ELSE User has scanned oLPN with matching container type and user continues to Palletize oLPNs . Process Change/Addition Allowing Different Container Types onto the Same Pallet System Code Mock Up System code enablement will appear similar to the below and will determine the logic behind which container types can be palletized together. First, to accommodate the desire to allow certain container types to be considered equivalent to others for the purpose of allowing them to be palletized together, a system code is set up where each parameter description will contain the additional container types and the parameter value contains the primary container type value signifying to WM that they can be palletized together. WM updates EX95 custom tracking screens for all equivalent container types upon the palletization of the first container on the pallet. In the below example, where a BLK1 container types exists, WM allows for BLK2 container types to be palletized with BLK1 and vice versa. This allow s for BLK1 and BLK2 to be palletized together on the same pallet . Where a RED2, RED3 container type exists, WM considers these container types equivalent to RED1 for palletization and allow s multiple container types to be palletized on the same pallet. If custom system not set up at all, WMOS exits custom logic and continues with original EX95 logic processing. Fourth , the Custom System Code 95A-EQV parameter rows must be configured. WM t reat s each parameter row as if it was unique and independent of one another. If the custom code C-95A-EVQ parameter row of the needed container type (s) is configured correctly , then the custom functionality within this section is enabled and a llows for configured container types to be palletized together . If the C-95A-EVQ parameter row of the needed container type(s) is null or configured incorrectly , WMOS exits custom logic and continues with original EX95 logic processing. Last, as this compares Container Types against parameters configured in system codes, all system code IDs , system code parameter descriptions, and value boxes must be configured correct ly with configured C ontainer T ypes found in Container Types UI as the value set for system code’s parameter description and value bo x . I f configured parameter description and value box are correct then WM will proceed to allow multi container type palletization. MCLN will own all container type configurations needed for EX95 . If an existing container type is entered for both parameter description and valu e for equivalent palletization , then the equivalency of palletization will be applied to allow multi container type palletization. EX 95 – Active Palletiz ation oLPNS Base WMOS does not inform the user that the pallet is ready to close and needing to end palletization. RF Palletize oLPN is enhanced to allow a configurable value to allow McLane to configure/customize the Maximum # of oLPNs on a pallet based on division requests. WM will display a custom message “ M aximum oLPNs /Totes allowed on Pallet ” which will show to the floor user that no more oLPNs /totes can fit onto the pallet – signaling to the user to end the current pallet and start a new pallet with the remaining oLPNs needing to be palletized.

Additionally, McLane would like visibility on the number of oLPNs /Totes remaining on a shipment to be packed . WMOS is enhanced to allow user to invoke custom CTRL-KEY: CTRL- T to display a custom Pallet Tracking screen As part of the enhancement, WMOS displays the pallet tracking screen grouped by container type within a shipment using CTRL-KEY: CTRL-T . WMOS displays totals oLPNs needing to be pa lletized , already pa lletized oLPNs , the total number of pallets and the percentage of oLPNs palletized completed as part of additional screen changes. WMOS is customized to display custom Pallet tracking screens by container type within a shipment when user invokes custom control key from pallet prompt in Palletize oLPN transaction. The tracking screen should display: • Total number of totes to be palletized on the pallet. • Already palletized totes . • The total number of pallets needed for the shipment . • The percentage of oLPNs palletized . Additionally, WMOS is enhanced to allow users to invoke custom CTRL-KEY: CTRL-C to display custom Tracking screens based on the status of the oLPN /Tote and the oLPN (s)/Tote(s) that are in that status remaining in the container type of the shipment. WMOS also introduces a custom error message CUST9502: “Container Type does not match for Designated Pallet” to allow for only oLPNs with the same container type to be palletized onto the same pallet. The custom warning message is CUST9501: "Maximum oLPNs /Totes allowed on Pallet" . This message will be displayed when the pallet has exceeded the max tote value C-E95-MAX . This message indicate s to the floor user that no more oLPNs /totes can fit onto the current pallet . Once the custom message is accepted, then WMOS automatically end s the current pallet and then prompt s the user for the base pallet prompt screen . This will allow the user to begin a new pallet with the remaining oLPNs needing to be palletized.

During the RF Palletize oLPN process, there is second custom message which will give information to the user. The custom message is CUST9502: “Container Type does not match for Designated Pallet.” This message will be displayed when the scanned oLPN does not match the first oLPN scanned onto the pallet. This message indicates to the floor user that the GLP/Tote does not belong onto the pallet as there are differing container types. Once the custom message is accepted, WMOS then prompts the user for the base pallet prompt screen. This will allow the user to begin a new pallet or scan an already existing pallet with the oLPNs needing to be palletized correctly. Enablement The following section describes the custom configuration required to enable this portion of the extension. First, the global modifications flag must be enabled. This is controlled through the custom system code C000 code id 000 parameter byte 1-1. This parameter is configured as a Y/N flag. If the flag is set to ‘Y’, then WMOS continues to the next enablement validation. Otherwise, if this flag is set to ‘N’, the global extension control is turned off, and only base processing will occur. Second, the extension specific global flag must be enabled. This is controlled through the custom system code C000 code id EX95 byte 1-1. This parameter is configured as a Y/N flag. If the flag is set to ‘Y’, then WMOS continues to the custom processing below. Otherwise, if the flag is set to ‘N’, the extension specific control for EX95 is turned off, and no custom EX95 processing will occur. Third, the extension specific MAX value must be configured. This is controlled through the custom system code C-E95 code id MAX byte 1-2. This parameter is configured as an edit box. If the edit box is configured, then WMOS continues to the custom processing below. Otherwise, if the edit box is ‘NULL’ or ’0', and the extension specific MAX value for EX95 is turned off, and no custom EX95 processing will occur. Next, RF Palletize oLPNs Transaction Parameter 500-500 must be enabled. This parameter is configured as a Y/N flag. If the flag is set to ‘Y’, then WMOS continues with custom processing below and display CUST9501: "Maximum oLPNs /Totes allowed on Pallet" when applicable. Otherwise, if the flag is set to ‘N’, the extension specific control for EX95 is turned off, and no custom EX95 processing will occur. Next, RF Palletize oLPNs Transaction Parameter 50 2 -50 2 must be enabled.

Validations The following section describes the validations performed by WMOS when the custom palletize oLPN message is received.    WMOS leverages base RF Palletize oLPN program to validate the oLPN and pallet. WMOS performs base validations against received pallet number. WMOS also performs base validations for oLPN . ` IF the total count of oLPNs on the pallet is greater than the configured Maximum number of oLPNs allowed on the pallet. THEN WM throws custom warning message CUST9501: “Maximum oLPNs /Totes allowed on Pallet” and takes user back to the pallet prompt screen. ELSE User has not reached the maximum number of oLPNs and user continues to Palletize oLPNs . IF the scanned oLPNs container type is NOT the same as the first scanned oLPNs container type on the pallet THEN WM throws custom error message CUST9502: “Container Type does not match for Designated Pallet ” and takes user back to the pallet prompt screen. ELSE User has scanned oLPN with matching container type and user continues to Palletize oLPNs . Process Change/Addition Allowing Different Container Types onto the Same Pallet System Code Mock Up System code enablement will appear similar to the below and will determine the logic behind which container types can be palletized together. First, to accommodate the desire to allow certain container types to be considered equivalent to others for the purpose of allowing them to be palletized together, a system code is set up where each parameter description will contain the additional container types and the parameter value contains the primary container type value signifying to WM that they can be palletized together. WM updates EX95 custom tracking screens for all equivalent container types upon the palletization of the first container on the pallet. In the below example, where a BLK1 container types exists, WM allows for BLK2 container types to be palletized with BLK1 and vice versa. This allow s for BLK1 and BLK2 to be palletized together on the same pallet .
